rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Frances Sussna'S Motion For Nonmonetary And Monetary Sanctions For Discovery Misconduct Against Prime Old County L.P., Prime Old County Acquisition And Homan & Stone
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 18, 2015, Line 10, Plaintiff Frances Sussna's Motion For Nonmonetary And Monetary Sanctions For Discovery Misconduct Against Prime Old County L.P., Prime Old County Acquisition And Homan & Stone. This is a discovery matter and the court will not provide a court reporter. If the parties wish to have a reporter present, they shall meet and confer and agree upon one prior to the hearing. The Motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: Defendant Prime is bound by its verified written discovery responses and may not present evidence at trial to contradict those responses. CACI 204, as amended in Plaintiff's proposed order, shall be given to the jury re spoliation of evidence. Note the parties stipulated to this. Sanctions for $2,000 which is the estimated amount of time and costs wasted by Plaintiff attributable to the unprepared PMK deponent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Frances Sussna'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Stephen Sturla And Daniel Marc Bornstein And Exclusion Of Expert Testimony
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 18, 2015, Line 10, Plaintiff Frances Sussna's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Stephen Sturla And Daniel Marc Bornstein And Exclusion Of Expert Testimony. This is a discovery matter and the court will not provide a court reporter. If the parties wish to have a reporter present, they shall meet and confer and agree upon one prior to the hearing. The motion is granted as to document requests to Bornstein 18, 19 (if available) and 20. As to request 6: Bornstein must provide a list of all publications and, if not otherwise reasonably available to plaintiff from other sources, provide copies on request at plaintiff's expense. As to 21: Bornstein must provide documents sufficient to show what percentage of his annual income is derived from Bay Property Group. The motion granted as to document requests to Sturla: 3, 8, 18, 19, 20. As to 6, Sturla must provide a list of all publications and, if not otherwise reasonably available to plaintiff from other sources, provide copies on request at plaintiff's expense. Defendants failed to provide admissible evidence that production of the requested documents would be burdensome. Sanctions awarded against defendants in the amount of $3,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: NISSIM NINIO VS. WILLIAM AYOUBI, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Compelling Defendant William Ayoubis Attendance At Deposition And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, September 18, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF NISSIM NINIO'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant William Ayoubis Attendance At Deposition And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz,, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel the appearance at deposition is GRANTED. The defendant presented no facts justifying his non-appearance at the rescheduled deposition. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,160.00, payable within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com ,a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: POLARIS DEVELOPMENT, LLC VS. ALPHA MEDIA GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 18, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT ALPHA MEDIA GROUP, LLC, VDM GROUP, INC., VLAD MYASNYANKIN, GK GROUP, INC., YEVGENIY KHIZVER, TRACE CAPITAL HOLDINGS, INC., TIMOTHY EUN, GARY KAHN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part with leave to amend the third cause of action. Defendant portrays plaintiff's entire first amended complaint as a sham pleading. Plaintiff has failed to adequately plead the third cause of action for rescission based on economic duress. As defendant correctly points out, plaintiff has raised an entirely new set of facts for pleading economic duress and has failed to provide a sufficient explanation for how or why the new facts relate to the facts originally pled. Thus, the court will disregard the inconsistent allegations and read into the amended complaint the allegations of the original complaint. See Owens v. Kings Supermarket (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 379, 384. The third cause of action for economic duress, as stated in the original complaint, does not adequately plead facts establishing that Plaintiff had no reasonable alternative but to agree to an unfavorable contract. See CrossTalk Productions, Inc. v. Jacobson (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 631, 644. The demurrer is sustained as to the third cause of action with ten days leave to amend. Contrary to defendant's position, plaintiff has adequately pled the second cause of action for rescission based on fraudulent inducement. The plaintiff has provided adequate explanations for the differences between the facts alleged in the original complaint and the facts pled in the first amendment complaint. Because Polaris has stated a cause of action for rescission based on fraudulent inducement, the release in the MIPA is arguably invalid, and therefore, Polaris can maintain its breach of contract claims, fraud claim, breach of fiduciary duty claim, and accounting claim. Furthermore, the plaintiff has pled a derivative suit on behalf of the Alpha Media Group shareholders in compliance with Cal. Corp. Code 800. Additionally, the court will grant plaintiff with ten days leave to amend the complaint to add defendants Gene Khizver, GK Group, Inc., Garry Kann, and Trace Capital Holding, Inc. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MARIO PASTRANA VS. MCINTOSH ASSOCIATES D/B/A S.R. TRAVEL,A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax, Or, In The Alternative, To Strike Costs Claimed By Defendant Mcintosh Associats. D/B/A/ S.R. Travel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 18, 2015, Line 9, PLAINTIFF MARIO PASTRANA'S Motion To Tax, Or, In The Alternative, To Strike Costs Claimed By Defendant Mcintosh Associates DBA S.R. Travel. Off calendar as moot. On September 1, 2015, Defendant filed a Notice of Withdrawal of Costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
